reviews.title: Our child loves it!

This case is really cute & our son loves it. The only downside is how easily the legs on the back fall out. We’ve actually already lost one side of the stand. I also wish it was made with a slightly more durable material. I’m afraid we’ll have to end up replacing it within the next few weeks. It does protect his iPad though.

Update- This case ended up being extremely durable! It's been 5 months since we purchased the original case & I am now buying a replacement bc we loved it so much. I'm sure if you don't have a 5 year old that's as rough on electronics as ours is then yours will last longer! The first time around we ordered blue, but this time we decided on green. Both colors are very vibrant. Would recommend!

I am not a fan of furniture assembly and consider it only slightly more enjoyable than root canal surgery. It took me a little over an hour to get this together.some of the pieces have stickers while most do not. In my opinion they all should have stickers assuming they are the correct stickers.The end result is unimpressive. It is very low, too low to comfortably watch tv unless you are significantly height impaired or spend most of your time on the floor. The materials are not high quality. As is typical with composites the material split and flaked when the screws were installed. It says it holds up to sixty pounds but I would not put anything heavy or expensive on it. It is fine for things like peripherals, sat or cable boxes and the like but it is not ideal for tvc. It looks fairly decent when assembled. But the top tends to bow when anything weighty placed on it. If you are looking for a temporary solution that won't break the bank this will do. It is lightweight and reasonably attractive but it lacks long term quality and durability.

reviews.title: Low vision user
reviews.text: I recently turned 65 and asked for this as a birthday present because if you haven’t noticed people like UPS Amazon the post office anybody that’s delivering now basically dumps and runs because they don’t wanna talk to you more than 10 seconds because it adds to her day. I understand that but I may not know when something arrives at my house other than maybe a email or a text message coming from the person. This way it activates as soon as they pull into my driveway or walk past my trashcan if they’re on foot so I know immediately that there’s somebody outside so overall it’s excellent. So what’s the negative my next door door door neighbor has a dog who likes to get biscuits for me and he will come to the front door and set the alarm off but then I know he’s there.  But overall that’s a small price to pay. It’s an excellent product I truly love it and it has made my life so much easier keeps me aware of what’s going on in the outside world when I’m tied up here at the house and don’t know really what could be going on if that makes sense thank you for reading my review I think if you have somebody with low vision they will love this because it gives them just a little bit of notice that somebody may be knocking or pounding on the door or dumping and run a package . Have a good day thanks for reading this

reviews.title: Updated review. It does not work after a heavy rain.
reviews.text: Easy to set up and works well until there is a heavy rain. (I reduced my initial rating from 5 stars to 4 stars because of this issue.) It does start to work consistently again after a period of 3 - 8 hours of dry weather. I still stand behind this product and do recommend this product for anyone who has a long and/or hidden view driveway. I have a monitor in the house and I have purchased a 2nd monitor to put in the barn. The 1500 ft range from the sensor to the monitor works well for us because our driveway is 1/4 mile from the barn to the road. As long as there is not heavy rain, it works well.

reviews.title: Works well for sleeping
reviews.text:
I wanted some headphones that were flat and wouldn't hurt my ears or fall out and get lost while I was trying to go to sleep. These work just fine. The speakers lie flatly against my head and don't poke or feel lumpy. I only use them for sleeping, though, not for exercising, so I can't say how easy or difficult it would be to remove the speakers and controller to wash the headband part.

Sound is fine. They don't go into your ear like regular earbuds, so you have to turn the volume up a little, but it does what it should. I haven't tried to control the volume using the buttons on the headband so I can't really rate that aspect. All in all, it does what I wanted it to.

reviews.title: Apalling image/text quality
reviews.text:
Now, in all fairness, I probably just spoiled myself rotten when I bought a $5000.00 Eizo professional monitor 8 or 10 years ago. Prior to that I'd been using two much less-expensive Samsung monitors and had been perfectly happy with them (excepting their longevity - neither of them lasted more than two or three years before going completely dead). Then, as I was embarking on a career as a pro photographer, I decided I needed a professional-quality monitor with precise and consistent color reproduction, so after hemming and hawing over the price for a while, I bit the proverbial bullet and laid down the cash for a new Eizo ColorEdge CG276. And honestly, at that time, I noticed that what I saw on my screen looked very nice, but I don't recall it blowing my mind - proof that the way to *really* appreciate what you've got is to do without it. Because I've now tried replacing this Eizo monitor (which is still working perfectly in every respect and which I'm still using and writing this review on now) with less expensive monitors twice, and each time I've shaken my head in disbelief that anybody could use a monitor with such crappy resolution and image quality, let alone think it was good.

That, sadly, but I guess as is to be expected, was my experience with this Samsung.

It didn't matter that the screen was curved; what I saw on the screen looked so terrible that it wouldn't matter if the monitor was a freaking planetarium, or a perfect globe surrounding me on all sides while I floated in the center via...I don't know...jetpack or something. I'd just have that much more poorly rendered graphics to look at.

I've attached five photos: one of the progress bar as it appears on my Eizo monitor, and two as it appears on this Samsung, followed by one of the Apple logo as it appears on the Eizo and finally, the Apple logo as it appears on the Samsung. You be the judge. (UPDATE: I just noticed that Amazon's website doesn't display the images in the same order in which you upload them (or at least it isn't with my images)...so suffice it to say that the really smooth and nice-looking progress bar and text and Apple logo are the Eizo's screen, and the really jaggy, glary, awful-looking ones are the Samsung's.)

I don't have the technical understanding of monitors required to explain which monitor-related variable it is, exactly, that makes the image quality on this Samsung so unsatisfactory (resolution? pixel size/shape? dunno.), but something definitely does it.

Maybe once you've used an Eizo on a Mac, you can never go back. So be it. If I really need a new monitor I'll just save my pennies until I can afford another Eizo...or at least something with much better specs than this Samsung. Because boy, was it a disappointment.

Ah well...lesson learned (kinda...even though I don't really know *why* I thought the Samsung sucked). I've boxed it back up and arranged my hassle-free return through Amazon and will at least get my money back...the return policy and process being two reasons I buy through Amazon in the first place. And I'll simply content myself to go on using a top-quality flat screen (that I can smoothly and easily raise and lower and pivot and even turn from landscape to portrait and back!) instead of trying to swap it out for a vastly inferior curved one (that I can only tilt up and down).

reviews.title: I do not recommend these earbuds.  They have a serious design flaw.
reviews.text:
I would have easily given these earbuds 4 or 5 stars, if it wasn't for the "low battery" warning. JLAB clearly put no thought into this. Here are my issues:

1) The first low battery warning occurs about 5 minutes before the battery dies . So if you just started your jog, or just got on the bus, you're screwed. With 6 hours of play time, the low battery notification should give you at least 20 minutes notice. Since you are almost always "out and about" when listening to headphones, it would be nice to have enough juice to finish your activity or get where you are going.

2) The low battery message (which is verbal) repeats FREQUENTLY for the brief remainder of your battery life, constantly interrupting your music. This is so annoying you wind up taking the earbuds off.

Since the earbuds are essentially useless once you get the first low battery warning, the warning serves no purpose. Giving the low battery warnings at the 30, 20 and 10 minute marks would be far less annoying and actually give you a chance to get to a charger.

reviews.title: Light Case/Sleep Wake Cover? Look no further.
reviews.text:
When I was looking for a case for my iPad Pro I wanted 2 things. I wanted 1.) Light weight 2.) Wake/Lock cover and this case has both.

I had never tried anything by Moko, but was pleasantly surprised by the quality. The case is super light and has served its purpose. Logo is very nice in the right bottom corner as it is not too big. Very happy with the magnet strength, it is strong enough to hold the iPad closed when holding upside down and if you want to make the iPad stand in landscape mode.

Case cutouts:
-Port for charger is very large and should work with 3rd party chargers.
-Port for 3.5mm headphone jack is large and should cause no concern for any headphones.
-Audio quality is unaffected and the cutouts on it look very nice. I enclosed a picture for you all to see.

The pictures have been after a few months of use. So the top cover has not shown much wear at all. Texture of the cover (Front and back) also feels good on the hand when holding.

Pain point: Cannot remove the smart cover, but that is expected when you buy this case. Makes taking pictures kinda awkward.

My Amazon Review Criteria:
5 Star: I got what I expected from the product and it was/worked better than expected. If you are considering a purchase, buy it. Would buy it again, I highly recommend.

4 Star: I got what I expected from the product and that is all. It just works. Still recommend the Item, probably hard to find a better product than this. Would buy again, I highly recommend.

3 Star: I got what I expected from the product most of the time. Has a few problems but it can be overlooked. Might buy the product again, but will look for other alternatives as well. I recommend the product, there might be better alternatives.

2 Star: I barely got what I expected from the product. Has many problems, product is bad. Would not buy again. I do not recommend buying this product unless there are no other alternatives.

1 Star: I never got what I expected from the product. Would not buy the product again, requested a refund or returned the product. (This is very rare for me.) I do not recommend buying this product whatsoever.

Note: I did NOT receive any incentive buying or reviewing this product. I want people to find products they like and will use.

reviews.title: Nice case
reviews.text: I recently got a MacBook air a2337 and wanted something that would protect my MacBook from getting scratches and to protect the keyboard.  It is a little tricky to put the top on but if you put the part that is close to the part where it meets the keyboard first its easier to snap it on.  It fits this model perfectly.  I saw a lot of reviews that say it didn't fit but it fits an a2337 perfectly.  The keyboard cover doesn't stick onto the keyboard which I guess is good lol I don't know what I expected but it will help keep dust off as well as any oils from fingertips.  I don't think it would protect the MacBook too well if it was dropped but is good to keep it from getting scratches from everyday wear.  The screen protector is a little tricky I would suggest using a ruler or a flat edge to go across from the middle to keep it from getting bubbles.  Would recommend getting this and a padded case to keep it in.

reviews.title: OK but could be a much better design
reviews.text:
First, it works as expected in that it replaces your battery (it is a battery eliminator not a charger) and powers your radio from a 12 volt accessory socket.

A couple poor design features to be aware of though:

The cord is a coiled cord, but the coils are very stiff. At rest it is about 2 feet long, but if you try to stretch it out, by the time you get to three feet it is pulling out of the car socket. I would say that for most people, they would have a lot of trouble talking on the radio with that limitation combined with the fact that at that length you are playing tug of war if it stays plugged in.

Also the cord comes out of the bottom of the battery adapter, which means you cannot stand your radio up. Not a concern for car use but in emergency use where you might have to be on 12 volt power indoors, you cannot stand up your radio. This means your antenna is operating at well below maximum efficiency.

It seems that just a little forethought and beta testing of this product would have made it a MUCH better product with little or no change to cost.
